Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have an event log stored in a DuckDB table named event_log with each row representing a single event columns at least case_id activity and timestamp Each case_id groups events that belong to one process instance or case The query should filter out any cases containing more than six events returning only the events from cases that have six or fewer events in total In other words we need to count number of events per case id and then exclude those with count greater than 6 then return full set for remaining ones

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to craft a query We are allowed to use subqueries or join with aggregated counts

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will likely do something like select e from event_log as e join select case_id from event_log group by case_id having count less than or equal to 6 valid_cases on e case_id equals valid_cases case_id That is the solution

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to consider the correct grouping and filtering to achieve this result That query should be written with where or group by having count events less than or equal to 6 In addition Ill check that the table name is event_log

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Alternatively we can use a window function in DuckDB if needed

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively if Im using window functions SELECT FROM SELECT count OVER PARTITION BY case_id as event_count from event_log subquery WHERE event_count less than or equal to 6 But since the problem says craft a DuckDB SQL query both ways are valid if supported by DuckDB version

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: I can also use exists but that might be less efficient so join is simpler
